message:
parity(views): satisfy Mapping protocol on Graph/DiGraph EdgeView (franken_networkx-mzj2)

dict(G.edges) silently produced {u: v} because the raw EdgeView had no
keys() method — dict() fell back to unpacking each 2-tuple yielded by
__iter__ as a (k, v) pair. Patch _EDGE_VIEW_TYPE (Rust type backing
Graph.edges) and _DiGraphEdgeView (Python wrapper class backing
DiGraph.edges) with the existing _adjacency_view_get/keys/items/values
helpers. __iter__ already yields (u, v) tuples and __getitem__ already
accepts a 2-tuple edge key, so the helpers give dict() the keys()+getitem
path it needs to produce {(u, v): attrs}.

test_adj_mapping_parity adds test_edges_satisfies_mapping_protocol covering
dict() equivalence, keys/items/values lists, and get() default behavior
against upstream networkx for both Graph and DiGraph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,6 +46,35 @@ def test_adj_get_matches_upstream_defaults(fnx_ctor):
     assert dict(fg.adj.get(1)) == {2: {}}
 
 
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("fnx_ctor", "nx_ctor"),
+    [
+        (fnx.Graph, nx.Graph),
+        (fnx.DiGraph, nx.DiGraph),
+    ],
+)
+def test_edges_satisfies_mapping_protocol(fnx_ctor, nx_ctor):
+    fg = fnx_ctor()
+    fg.add_edge("a", "b", weight=3)
+    fg.add_edge("b", "c", weight=5)
+    ng = nx_ctor()
+    ng.add_edge("a", "b", weight=3)
+    ng.add_edge("b", "c", weight=5)
+
+    for attr in ("items", "keys", "values", "get"):
+        assert hasattr(fg.edges, attr), f"{fnx_ctor.__name__}.edges is missing {attr}"
+
+    assert dict(fg.edges) == dict(ng.edges)
+    assert list(fg.edges.keys()) == list(ng.edges.keys())
+    assert list(fg.edges.items()) == list(ng.edges.items())
+    assert list(fg.edges.values()) == list(ng.edges.values())
+    assert fg.edges.get(("a", "b")) == ng.edges.get(("a", "b"))
+    assert fg.edges.get(("x", "y")) is ng.edges.get(("x", "y")) is None
+    assert fg.edges.get(("x", "y"), "sentinel") == ng.edges.get(
+        ("x", "y"), "sentinel"
+    )
+
+
 def test_reverse_view_adj_exposes_mapping_helpers():
     fg = fnx.DiGraph()
     fg.add_edges_from([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
